clinical_history: Extra Credit:  74-year-old woman with syncope with multiple major findings, one immediately life-threatening.
diagnosis: Complex ECG showing:  1) Underlying atrial fibrillation; 2) Long Q-T(U); 3) Ventricular pacing with intermittent sensing failure; and 4) Run of torsade de pointes-type of polymorphic ventricular tachycardia (second half of record).
